heading: Lead system integrator; financial interests
label: 396 Lead system integrator; financial interests
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans’ Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act, 2007, and not as part of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: With respect to contracts entered into after , and except as provided in subsection (b), no entity performing lead system integrator functions in the acquisition of a major system by the Department of Homeland Security may have any direct financial interest in the development or construction of any individual system or element of any system of systems.July 1, 2007

children.label: A
children.text: the entity was selected by the Department of Homeland Security as a contractor to develop or construct the system or element concerned through the use of competitive procedures; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Department took appropriate steps to prevent any organizational conflict of interest in the selection process; or
the entity was selected by a subcontractor to serve as a lower-tier subcontractor, through a process over which the entity exercised no control.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: c Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to preclude an entity described in subsection (a) from performing work necessary to integrate two or more individual systems or elements of a system of systems with each other.

children.heading: Regulations update
children.label: d Regulations update
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary of Homeland Security shall update the acquisition regulations of the Department of Homeland Security in order to specify fully in such regulations the matters with respect to lead system integrators set forth in this section. Included in such regulations shall be: (1) a precise and comprehensive definition of the term “lead system integrator”, modeled after that used by the Department of Defense; and (2) a specification of various types of contracts and fee structures that are appropriate for use by lead system integrators in the production, fielding, and sustainment of complex systems.July 1, 2007
